title: Designing Token Budgets for Long-Context Agents
author: Sample Author
content:
# Designing Token Budgets

When agents have to work over long horizons, the dominant cost driver is not raw model price but **context refresh frequency**.

## Why budgets matter

Builders who skip explicit budgets end up rebuilding the same observation/action history every turn.

## Two strategies

1. Sliding window with eviction
2. Episodic checkpoint with rehydration

highlights.text: the dominant cost driver is not raw model price but context refresh frequency
Builders who skip explicit budgets end up rebuilding the same observation/action history every turn
highlights.note: 私人批注：这里和 ReAct 的 step-level checkpoint 一致。

title: Approval Outcomes as First-Class Domain Objects
author: Another Author
content:
# Approval Outcomes

Most knowledge tools collapse human review into a single yes/no flag.
First-class outcomes (reject, defer, append, link, merge, split) preserve user intent for later compaction.

highlights.text: Most knowledge tools collapse human review into a single yes/no flag
First-class outcomes preserve user intent for later compaction
